docs.text: Welcome to Kiyu's Blog! CP (Competitive Programming) is a mind sport which requires participants to write efficient and correct programs to solve problems. I wrote this blog to make it a free and helpful resource for anyone interest in CP. The idea of dividing categories into intro, intermediate, and veteran comes from the club of my school: UAPSPC I am really grateful to be in CP community and hope this helps you too, enjoy.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: About This is a personal archive for competitive programming related materials. Not all codes are guaranteed to be completely correct to use in commercial projects or programming competitions. 11 Rippuru bilibili home page

docs.text: Intro Session This session will cover some of the most basic algorithms and data structures in programming competitions. At the end of each page, there will also be a link to helpful resources, and related exercises.
docs.title: Overview

docs.text: This session will cover some of the most basic algorithms and data structures in programming competitions. At the end of each page, there will also be a link to helpful resources, and related exercises.
docs.title: Intro Session

docs.text: C++ in Competitive Programming C++ is the most used language in CP because of its efficiency. So, I will be mostly using C++ in sample codes but the thoughts apply to any other prgramming languages. Knowledge on pointer, struct , and class are not required for competitive programming. Resource Sololearn : free tutorial Learn C++ : free tutorials that I used General Template This template is what I personally use to shorten my code and also speed up I/O (input and output). When the size of problem \(n\) becomes larger than 1M, I/O can be an obstacle in the program's runtime. In these scenarios, speeding up I/O is really necessary to pass in the given time. Templates can also save your time in practice so you don't have to type everything down before actually solving the problem 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 #include <bits/stdc++.h> using namespace std ; using ll = long long ; using vi = vector < int > ; int main () { cin . tie ( 0 ) -> sync_with_stdio ( 0 ); // speed up I/O } Resource KACTL : macros/typedefs defined in the template that help shorten the code. Benjamin Qi's template : a much longer one, involves more macro and self-defined functions USACO Guide: Exaplanation on <bits/stdc++.h> Tools to use In CP, we often use light-weighted IDE or even editor like Vim / Sublime Text. Heavy weighted IDE like Visual Studio or Eclipse can be used but they are designed for larger projects and can be an overkill since the whole program will be in a single source file. Often used tools include: Vim / Emacs : classic editor, also check Neovim, Macvim. Sublime Text 3 : fast, lightweight, free to use. Geany : lightweight VSCode : PAP and VSCode Docs CP Editor : editor designed for CP. Resource tmwilliamlin168's vim config STL Standard template library is one of the reasons why C++ is widely used in CP. C++ STL offers many helpful functions and data types. These functions and data types prevent us from implementing a BST in real contest mannually so it is helpful to take a look at STL while learning some of the basic algorithms. Resource Geeks for Geeks C++ Reference
docs.title: C++ in CP
